sections.heading: What's a tree?
sections.body: A **tree** is data that branches. There's a **root** at the top, and every node can have **children**. A node with no children is a **leaf**. Unlike a real tree, computer science trees grow downward.

sections.title: Your family tree
sections.body: You at the top. Your two parents below. Their parents below that. Each person has zero or more children. The structure branches. That's exactly a tree in code.

sections.caption: A binary tree — each node has at most two children

questions.question: A node with no children is called a:
questions.explanation: Leaves are nodes at the end of branches — no children below them.
